summary: Repair two audit failures in signal-sweep blog monitor: (1) seed_blog_urls.sql references columns (path, is_active, created_at) that do not exist on the actual vendor.blog table — rewrite to use source_url parsed into domain/path, is_accessible, original_created_at; (2) change_bridge.py bridge_to_article_input() returns title=None, content=None, source='field_monitor' which fails ingest_article() validation — add 'field_monitor' to ArticleSource enum and change bridge to return source='company_blog' with placeholder title/content that triggers fetch-first ingestion path.

fixes.problem: References vendor.blog columns that do not exist: path, is_active, created_at

actual_columns.source_url: TEXT (exists, parse path from this via regexp_replace)

fixes.action: Rewrite SELECT to: domain from vb.domain, path from regexp_replace(vb.source_url, '^https?://[^/]+', ''), filter on vb.is_accessible = true, order by vb.original_created_at DESC NULLS LAST

fixes.problem: bridge_to_article_input() returns title=None, content=None, source='field_monitor'. ingest_article() rejects None for required fields title/content and 'field_monitor' is not a valid ArticleSource enum value.
fixes.action: Change source to 'company_blog' (valid ArticleSource enum). Set title to '[Signal Sweep] {domain}{path}' as placeholder. Set content to '[Pending fetch — content_hash changed from {old_hash[:8]} to {new_hash[:8]}]' as placeholder. The ingest layer validates non-None and non-empty — these placeholders satisfy that while clearly marking the record as fetch-pending.
fixes.alternative_considered: Adding 'field_monitor' to ArticleSource enum was rejected — that would modify an existing protected file (ingest_article.py) for a source type that isn't a true article source. 'company_blog' is semantically correct since these are company blog URLs.

fixes.problem: bridge_to_article_input() does not pass old_hash/new_hash into the returned dict's top level for downstream tracing
fixes.action: No change needed — metadata block already carries field_id, url_id, old_hash, new_hash. This is informational only.

constraints: Do not expand scope beyond the three allowed_paths
Do not modify ingest_article.py — use existing ArticleSource enum values
Do not modify existing movement_engine files
Preserve all kill switches and sample gates
Seed SQL must remain idempotent (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING)
verification: seed_blog_urls.sql executes without error against marketing DB
